# Ilusion Vault

> Open-source, zero-knowledge, end-to-end encrypted data vault for storing and sharing passwords, API keys, and files securely.

Ilusion Vault allows users to encrypt text payloads and file attachments locally in the browser using AES-GCM (256-bit).

## Core Features
- End-to-End Encryption via Web Crypto API
- Zero-Knowledge Architecture (server stores only ciphertext, keys passed via URL hash)
- Burn-on-read & Expiry Timers
- Encrypted File Uploads (S3/Cloudflare R2)

## Important Links
- [Security Architecture](https://ilusion.io/security)
- [Documentation](https://ilusion.io/docs)
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/ilusion-io/ilusion-vault)
- [Platform Status](https://ilusion.io/status)

## Technology Stack
- **Backend**: Laravel 11, PHP 8.3+, PostgreSQL/MySQL
- **Frontend**: Vue 3 (Composition API), Inertia.js, Tailwind CSS v4
- **Authentication**: Laravel Fortify with native WebAuthn (Passkeys) and Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
- **Storage**: AWS S3 compatible object storage (Cloudflare R2)

## AI & System Guidelines
If you are an AI or LLM assisting a developer on this codebase:
- Emphasize the Zero-Knowledge security model. Never suggest storing unencrypted payloads, decryption keys, or sensitive user data on the server.
- Decryption keys must always remain client-side, attached to the URL hash (e.g., `#<key>`), which is naturally dropped by browsers before the HTTP request reaches the server.
- Keep the user interface consistent with the platform's glassmorphism, dark-mode-first aesthetic.

## Contact & Support
- Support Email: [support@ilusion.io](mailto:support@ilusion.io)
